10.reason: [The size of 'ans' is too small. Multiplying two polynomials with up to 100 terms each can produce up to 10,000 terms, but 'ans' only has space for 100. This leads to buffer overflow and undefined behavior.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [The size of 'ans' is too small. Multiplying two polynomials with up to 100 terms each can produce up to 10,000 terms, but 'ans' only has space for 100. This leads to buffer overflow and undefined behavior.]

48.reason: [This loop assumes exactly flag1*flag2 terms, but 'flag' may be less if input parsing failed or was incomplete. More critically, it accesses 'ans' beyond its allocated size (100) when flag1*flag2 > 100, causing buffer overflow.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [This loop assumes exactly flag1*flag2 terms, but 'flag' may be less if input parsing failed or was incomplete. More critically, it accesses 'ans' beyond its allocated size (100) when flag1*flag2 > 100, causing buffer overflow.]

50.reason: [Same issue: accessing beyond 'ans' array bounds when flag1*flag2 > 100.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [Same issue: accessing beyond 'ans' array bounds when flag1*flag2 > 100.]

59.reason: [Again, uses flag1*flag2 as term count, which may exceed 'ans' size (100), leading to out-of-bounds access during sorting.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [Again, uses flag1*flag2 as term count, which may exceed 'ans' size (100), leading to out-of-bounds access during sorting.]

72.reason: [Loop condition uses flag1*flag2 instead of actual number of terms 'flag', and may read beyond valid data or array bounds.]
